sections:
Export packing is a discipline in its own right, and it sits a long way from the cardboard-and-tape approach that works perfectly well for a move down the road. When your belongings are bound for a container ship, an air-freight pallet or a long road haul across the Continent, they must survive weeks of handling, stacking, vibration, humidity swings and rough crane work before anyone opens the doors at the far end. Our export packing service is built around that reality. Every item we prepare is wrapped, cushioned and contained to recognised international and marine freight standards, so it arrives in the same condition it left your home in West Sussex. As a family-run firm based at Doryln House on the London Road in Ashington near Pulborough, we have been preparing consignments for overseas shipment since 2016, and we treat each load as if it were our own crossing the ocean.

The core principle is simple — assume the worst handling conditions and pack accordingly. That means heavier-grade materials, fuller cushioning, sealed barriers against moisture, and crates built to take weight from above. It also means a meticulous inventory and the right paperwork, because a beautifully packed shipment is no use if it is held at customs. If you are coordinating a wider relocation, our export packing dovetails with our international removals service so packing, freight and delivery are handled by one accountable team.
The Materials We Use And Why They Matter

Materials make or break an export consignment, and we do not cut corners on them. We use double-wall and, where appropriate, triple-wall cartons that hold their shape under compression, because freight is stacked high and a collapsing box takes everything below it with it. For surfaces and finishes we use acid-free tissue and clean newsprint against the item, then bubble wrap and foam in layers so nothing rubs through. Moisture is the silent enemy of any sea shipment, so we reach for vapour-barrier wrapping, shrink film and, on longer crossings, desiccant pouches that draw dampness out of the enclosed space.

Each material has a job, and we match it to the contents rather than wrapping everything the same way. A typical export pack from our team will draw on the following:

  • Double and triple-wall export cartons rated to hold weight in a stack
  • Acid-free tissue and clean paper for direct contact with polished and painted surfaces
  • Multi-layer bubble wrap, foam sheet and edge protectors for corners and projections
  • Vapour-barrier film and desiccant for sea freight and tropical destinations
  • Custom timber crates, plinths and battens for the heaviest and most delicate pieces
  • Strong export-grade tape, banding and corner guards to keep everything closed and square

For pieces that need more than a carton can offer, we move up to timber. Our custom crate service builds bespoke cases around mirrors, marble, sculpture, electronics and anything awkward, so the structure itself takes the strain rather than the contents.
How We Prepare A Consignment Step By Step

Good export packing is a process, not a single action, and we follow the same disciplined sequence on every job. It begins with a survey, ideally in your home, where we assess what is travelling, how it is built, what it is made from and which items need crating rather than boxing. From that we plan the materials, the order of packing and the loading sequence, because the way a container is filled affects how the contents fare in transit.

On packing day we wrap each item individually before it ever touches a box, building up cushioning so there is no movement inside the carton. Heavy and rigid items are crated; fragile, high-value and irregular pieces get bespoke protection. As we go, we photograph and log everything, building the inventory that travels with the shipment. Finally we seal, label and band each unit, recording weights and dimensions so the freight side knows exactly what it is carrying. Where the contents call for the same level of care that does not need a crate, our fragile packing service and our wider full packing service use the identical professional methods, just scaled to the items in front of us.
Inventories, Customs Documentation And Paperwork Support

Export packing is only half the story — the paperwork has to match. A clear, itemised inventory is the backbone of any international shipment. It tells the freight forwarder what is in the load, gives customs a description of the goods, and protects you if a query ever arises. We compile a detailed inventory as we pack, numbering and photographing items so each carton and crate can be cross-referenced against the list. That same record makes unpacking at the far end straightforward, because everything is identified and accounted for.

We also support the documentation side of your move. While your chosen freight forwarder or shipping agent handles the formal customs declarations, we provide the packing list, weights, dimensions and item descriptions they need to complete them accurately, and we flag anything that commonly attracts customs attention so there are no surprises at the border. Getting these details right keeps your consignment moving and avoids costly holds. For moves within Europe, our European removals service brings the same documentation discipline to road freight across the Continent.
Who Needs Export Packing And When

Export packing is for anyone whose belongings are crossing a border or an ocean, whether that is a few treasured pieces or an entire household. Families emigrating, professionals taking an overseas posting, retirees moving to a home in the sun, and collectors shipping antiques or art all rely on it. Antique dealers and private buyers are a regular part of our work given our LAPADA membership and the trust placed in us by firms such as Fine & Country, Justin Lloyd and Mansell McTaggart, who recommend us for exactly this kind of careful, high-value handling.

You should be thinking about export packing the moment a sea or air shipment is on the cards, because lead times for surveys, crate building and materials all need planning. The general rule is straightforward — if an item is going by container, air pallet or long-distance road freight, it needs export-grade preparation rather than standard removal packing. If you are unsure how much you are shipping and whether some of it might travel later, our storage calculator helps you size the consignment, and our secure storage can hold items between packing and shipping dates.
Why Choose Wolves Removals For Export Packing

Experience and accountability are what separate a shipment that arrives intact from one that does not. We are a family-run Sussex company, founded in 2016, covering West Sussex, East Sussex, Surrey, Hampshire and Kent alongside UK-wide and international moves. We are Checkatrade-verified, a member of LAPADA, and fully insured with liability cover up to £10 million, so your consignment is protected through every stage of preparation. When you deal with us you deal with the same people from survey to seal, which means nothing falls between the cracks.

We bring genuine first-hand expertise to every export job, the right materials in stock, and crate-building skills in-house rather than subcontracted out. That combination keeps quality consistent and timescales tight. To get started, you can request a tailored estimate through our get a quote page, review our transparent pricing, learn more about our family business, or read about how we serve clients across West Sussex. Call us on 01903 893731 or 07789 390421, or email contact@wolves-removals.co.uk, and we will plan a packing solution built for the journey ahead.
faqs: What is the difference between export packing and normal removal packing?

Normal removal packing protects belongings for a short, controlled journey by van. Export packing assumes weeks of rough handling, stacking, vibration and humidity in a container or on a freight pallet. It uses heavier-grade cartons, fuller cushioning, moisture barriers, desiccant and bespoke timber crates, plus a detailed inventory and documentation support. It is the standard your goods need for sea, air or long-distance road freight.
Do you provide the timber crates for fragile and high-value items?

Yes. We build bespoke timber crates in-house through our custom crate service, sized around the individual item so the case takes the load rather than the contents. This is ideal for mirrors, marble, artwork, electronics, antiques and anything heavy or awkward heading overseas.
Can you help with customs paperwork for an international shipment?

We provide the detailed packing list, item descriptions, weights and dimensions that your freight forwarder or shipping agent needs to complete the formal customs declarations, and we flag items that commonly attract customs attention. Combined with the numbered, photographed inventory we compile as we pack, this keeps your shipment moving smoothly through the border.
Which areas do you cover for export packing?

We are based at Doryln House, London Road, Ashington, near Pulborough RH20 3JT, and cover West Sussex, East Sussex, Surrey, Hampshire and Kent, alongside UK-wide and European or international moves. Wherever you are in the region, we can survey your home, prepare your consignment and coordinate it with our international removals service.
